how_it_works.heading: How Flexible Routing Works
how_it_works.content: WARP builds routes daily based on available freight density, delivery windows, and vehicle capacity. Routes are optimized in real-time as pickups confirm and volumes fluctuate throughout the day.

warp_implementation.heading: How WARP Uses Flexible Routing
points: Dynamic route building based on daily freight density
Multi-stop optimization that adjusts to real-time volume
Same-day route changes without rebilling complexity

when_to_use.heading: When to Use Flexible Routing
when_to_use.content: Best for multi-stop delivery programs where stop counts and volumes vary daily. Particularly effective for store replenishment and vendor consolidation where flexibility reduces empty miles.

solutions.label: Store Replenishment
solutions.description: Scheduled multi-stop retail freight delivery using LTL and right-sized assets

solutions.label: Vendor Consolidation
solutions.description: Multi-vendor pickup consolidation into single linehaul shipments

equipment.label: Cargo Van
equipment.description: Enclosed cargo van for small-freight and last-mile delivery

equipment.label: Box Truck
equipment.description: Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes

concepts.label: Cross-Docking
concepts.description: Direct transfer of freight between inbound and outbound vehicles without warehousing

concepts.label: Middle Mile
concepts.description: The linehaul segment between origin hub and destination hub before final delivery

concepts.label: Scan-Level Visibility
concepts.description: Item-level or pallet-level tracking with scan events at every handling point

concepts.label: Right-Sized Assets
concepts.description: Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

items.q: What is flexible routing in freight?
items.a: Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements
WARP builds routes daily based on available freight density, delivery windows, and vehicle capacity. Routes are optimized in real-time as pickups confirm and volumes fluctuate throughout the day.

items.q: When should a shipper use flexible routing?
items.a: Best for multi-stop delivery programs where stop counts and volumes vary daily. Particularly effective for store replenishment and vendor consolidation where flexibility reduces empty miles.

items.q: How does WARP implement flexible routing?
items.a: Dynamic route building based on daily freight density. Multi-stop optimization that adjusts to real-time volume. Same-day route changes without rebilling complexity.

items.q: What metrics matter for flexible routing?
items.a: Key performance metrics include stops per route, miles per stop, route density, on-time delivery rate.

rendered.body_text:
Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements

WARP builds routes daily based on available freight density, delivery windows, and vehicle capacity. Routes are optimized in real-time as pickups confirm and volumes fluctuate throughout the day.

Applies to LTL, Cargo Van / Box Truck freight operations. Learn how WARP implements this at wearewarp.com/quote.
rendered.primary_content_html:
What Is Flexible Routing?

Dynamic route optimization based on real-time freight density and delivery requirements

Applies to: LTL, Cargo Van / Box Truck

How Flexible Routing Works

WARP builds routes daily based on available freight density, delivery windows, and vehicle capacity. Routes are optimized in real-time as pickups confirm and volumes fluctuate throughout the day.

How WARP Uses Flexible Routing

  • Dynamic route building based on daily freight density
  • Multi-stop optimization that adjusts to real-time volume
  • Same-day route changes without rebilling complexity
  • Pickup and delivery window flexibility within committed SLAs

When to Use Flexible Routing

Best for multi-stop delivery programs where stop counts and volumes vary daily. Particularly effective for store replenishment and vendor consolidation where flexibility reduces empty miles.

Flexible Routing Performance Metrics

  • Stops Per Route
  • Miles Per Stop
  • Route Density
  • On-Time Delivery Rate

Related Solutions & Capabilities

Solutions

  • Store Replenishment — Scheduled multi-stop retail freight delivery using LTL and right-sized assets
  • Vendor Consolidation — Multi-vendor pickup consolidation into single linehaul shipments

Equipment

  • Cargo Van — Enclosed cargo van for small-freight and last-mile delivery
  • Box Truck — Medium-duty box truck (16-26 ft) for mid-size loads and multi-stop routes

Related Concepts

  • Cross-Docking — Direct transfer of freight between inbound and outbound vehicles without warehousing
  • Middle Mile — The linehaul segment between origin hub and destination hub before final delivery
  • Scan-Level Visibility — Item-level or pallet-level tracking with scan events at every handling point
  • Right-Sized Assets — Matching vehicle type and capacity to actual shipment size for cost efficiency

Ship with Flexible Routing

Get Instant Quote Book a Fit Call
